durations.name: Instantaneous
durations.description: The jutsu harms, heals, creates, or alters a creature or object in a way that can't be dispelled — its effect exists only for an instant.

durations.name: Concentration
durations.description:
Requires the caster to maintain concentration to keep the effect active. The duration entry specifies how long concentration can be held; concentration can be ended at any time with no action required (unless otherwise specified). Maintaining a jutsu costs half (rounded down) of its initial chakra cost, paid at the start of each of the caster's turns or the jutsu ends.

Up to two concentration jutsu can be maintained at once; casting a third breaks one. Taking damage forces a Chakra Control check (DC 12 + sum of concentrated jutsu ranks, or half the damage taken — whichever is higher); a separate check is made per damage source. Becoming Incapacitated or dying ends concentration. The GM may also call for DC 20 Chakra Control checks for environmental disturbances.

durations.name: Round
durations.description: A round lasts until the beginning of the next turn of the creature that used the jutsu or feature. If the effect targets hostile creatures, it lasts until the beginning of each respective hostile's next turn. If no creature initiated the effect, it lasts until the start of the turn or initiative count when activated.
